t_ch: 大事不好了，我刚才收到信息，会长让我立刻停止码头升级。
t_en: There's been an unexpected change in plans — I've received word that the president has ordered me to stop all upgrades to the dock immediately.

t_ch: 会长知道码头升级有多重要吧？
t_en: Does he know how important it is to upgrade the dock?

t_ch: 我认为他不会不知道。
t_en: There's no way he couldn't know.

t_ch: 会长是公司派来的奸细吧？
t_en: Maybe he's an IPC spy?

t_ch: 现在可不是开玩笑的时候呀。
t_en: Now's not the time for jokes.

t_ch: 太好了，我现在就想摆烂了。
t_en: Maybe he knows when it's time to give up hope.

t_ch: 现在可不是开玩笑的时候呀。
t_en: Now's not the time for jokes.

t_ch: 我也不太理解，走吧，我们去找会长讨个说法。
t_en: I'm also confused. Let's go have a word with him.

t_ch: 我也给素裳姑娘发个信息，到陈机铺去汇合。
t_en: I sent a message to Sushang telling her to meet up at Oldies Depot.

t_ch: 正好，他们来了。
t_en: Good, they've arrived.

t_ch: 驭空大人也来了？
t_en: Madam Yukong, you're here too?

t_ch: 我有事要找金人巷商会商议，不过，你们先说吧。
t_en: I also have something I wish to discuss with the Merchant Guild, but I'll wait for you to finish.

t_ch: 会长呢，我要见会长！
t_en: We're going to pay a visit to the president!

t_ch: 我们还见不着会长吗？
t_en: Will we actually get to see him this time?

t_ch: 我懂了，会长一开始就不存在！
t_en: Wait, I get it... the president doesn't even exist!

t_ch: 什么，这倒是我没有想到。
t_en: What? I hadn't even considered something like that.

t_ch: 会长，你别躲在里面不出声。
t_en: President, get your butt out here!

t_ch: 我知道你在店里，你开门啊。
t_en: Yeah, we know you're in there. Open the door!

t_ch: 会长充分感谢各位付出的努力，也知道各位想问什么。
t_en: The president fully appreciates your efforts and knows what you want to ask.

t_ch: 他认为继续升级码头，这做法与公司没有本质差异，只会破坏金人巷的特色。
t_en: He believes that continuing to upgrade the dock, a strategy that is not fundamentally different from that of the IPC, will only destroy the unique character of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 金人巷是充满人情味的街道，无论是小吃街还是文玩旧货都是围绕当地居民与游客建设，这是金人巷的独特性与吸引力。
t_en: Aurum Alley is a place brimming with personality — from the delicious food to the cultural antiques. Everything is built around visiting tourists and local residents, and that's what makes it such a special place.

t_ch: 根据历史数据来看，继续物流开发会将这里变成「大型仓库」。物流越是提升，游客越是减少，这对金人巷的商铺和社区来说是不可接受的。
t_en: Based on historical data, continued development of the shipping operations will turn the area into a giant warehouse. The more the shipping capacity is increased, the less attractions there will be for visitors. Such an outcome is unacceptable for the shopkeepers and community of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: …我读书少，你可别骗我！真的是这样的吗？
t_en: ...Ugh, historical data isn't my forte. You better be telling the truth!

t_ch: 还是应该继续升级码头。
t_en: We have to keep upgrading the dock.

t_ch: 这件事应该问问商户们。
t_en: We should ask the merchants about this.

t_ch: 我赞同会长维护传统。
t_en: I agree with the president in upholding tradition.

t_ch: 正好，现在换我来讲了。
t_en: Very well, I believe it's my turn to speak.

t_ch: 天舶司收到了许多商铺的联名请求：「重组商会」。换句话说，商户们希望你能辞任商会会长一职。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission has received a joint request from several merchants concerning the reorganization of the Merchant Guild. Mr. President, the merchants would like you to resign as the president of the Guild.

t_ch: 金人巷的商户们…有一天也会背叛我吗？
t_en: There has come a day... that the merchants of Aurum Alley have betrayed me?

t_ch: 我不会放弃我为金人巷打造的图景，更别说卸下职责了。
t_en: I won't give up the vision I've built for Aurum Alley, let alone step down from my duties.

t_ch: 会长的声音吗？
t_en: Is that the president speaking?

t_ch: 我无意干预商会决策，但作为老友我要提醒你，金人巷永远是当下的，它不在过去。
t_en: It is not my intention to interfere with the Merchant Guild's affairs, but as an old friend I would like to remind you that Aurum Alley only exists in the present, not in the past.

t_ch: …驭空大人，现在总是由过去造就，不是吗？
t_en: ...Madam Yukong, the present is always made from the past, is it not?

t_ch: 我建议你做出一些让步，以缓和商铺的态度，比如采用明曦他们的做法，或是由明曦暂代你的职务。
t_en: I suggest that you make some concessions to appease the merchants. If you don't follow Mingxi's plans for the dock, she will temporarily take over your position.

t_ch: 明曦，我想听听你的想法。
t_en: Mingxi, I would like to hear your thoughts.

t_ch: …会长，我希望您理解，我绝不是为了掌控金人巷事务的权力而来。我自小听着会长描绘这儿的繁荣，也想为实现这幅图景努力。只是，现在您的想法和我们有了冲突。
t_en: ...President, I hope you understand that I am in no way vying for power over Aurum Alley. I have listened to the president's vision of prosperity since I was a child, and I want to work to achieve that vision. But currently, your ideas conflict with ours.

t_ch: 建议你听听驭空大人的建议。
t_en: I suggest you heed the advice of Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 给我一个机会，证明你是错的。
t_en: Give me a chance to prove you wrong.

t_ch: 我明白了。我可以接受明曦暂代我的职责。
t_en: I understand. I can accept Mingxi temporarily taking over my duties.

t_ch: 不过，明曦要将自己的决策在所有商铺面前公之于众，说服我，也赢得商铺的认可。你既然有信心，那这点应该能做到吧？
t_en: However, Mingxi must make her decision public, and win the approval of both myself and all the merchants. Since you have shown confidence, those terms should be acceptable to you?

t_ch: 嗯，我会全力以赴的，会长。
t_en: Yes, I'll do my best, President.

t_ch: 你们要记住，就算你们取代了我，危机还是迫在眉睫——你们至少得挣到「投标保证金」的数额，才有机会挫败公司。
t_en: You have to remember that even when you're acting in my place, a crisis is still imminent — you must earn at least the amount of the bidding deposit to even have a chance of thwarting off the IPC.

t_ch: 很好，既然会长已做出决定，便由我来负责召集公开会议。
t_en: Very well, since the president has come to a decision, I will be in charge of calling a public meeting.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，明曦，我有事找你们，等此间事毕，记得来找我一下。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Mingxi, I have something for you. When you are all finished here, remember to come and see me.

t_ch: …会长就在这里。明明自己也能说话，却要我帮他当传声筒，哎。
t_en: *sigh* ...I wish the president did more of the talking. I'm getting tired of being a mouthpiece.

t_ch: 这会长也太死板了。
t_en: *sigh* This President is kinda stubborn...

t_ch: 不过话又说回来，继续开发码头和公司真的没有区别吗？
t_en: Is there really no difference between our strategy and the IPC's?

t_ch: 公司的做法也不一定就是错的。
t_en: Just because the IPC does something doesn't mean it's wrong.

t_ch: 我听说银河中不少地区接受了公司的商业合作，并一举成为了星际贸易热门地区。
t_en: I've heard that many places in the galaxy that partnered with the IPC have become popular areas for interstellar trade.

t_ch: 有很多商铺反对本地商会、支持公司，也是合情合理的。
t_en: It makes sense that there are many merchants who oppose the Merchant Guild and support the IPC.

t_ch: 我们和他们，还是有些区别的。
t_en: There's a difference alright.

t_ch: 什么区别呢？
t_en: What kind of difference?

t_ch: 我们是为了金人巷而奔走？
t_en: Everything we do is for Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 你想说公司是为了公司做出的决策，我们是为金人巷做出的决策。
t_en: You're saying that the IPC is in it for the IPC, but we just want what's best for Aurum Alley...

t_ch: 但同样是为了金人巷，我们和会长也会产生分歧。
t_en: ...but the president wants what's best for Aurum Alley too, and yet we still disagree with him.

t_ch: …我们没有公司能挣钱？
t_en: We don't earn nearly as much money as the IPC?

t_ch: 难道就不应该选择能挣钱的那一方吗？
t_en: Shouldn't people choose the side that can earn them money?

t_ch: 街道的主人既不是商会，也不是公司…是生活在这里的人。
t_en: The streets aren't owned by the Merchant Guild or the IPC... but by those who live here.

t_ch: 我讲不出什么大道理，只是觉得，我们应该多问问商铺的意见……
t_en: *sigh* I guess I don't have any special insights. I think... I think we should ask the merchants what they think.

t_ch: 驭空大人有事找我们，走吧。
t_en: Madam Yukong is looking for us. Let's be on our way.

t_ch: 我听夕葵说，是你作为商业顾问协助金人巷商会重整旗鼓…真是多才多艺，还有什么事是你不会做的？
t_en: I heard from Xikui that it was you who became a business advisor and assisted Aurum Alley Merchant Guild in their dispute with the IPC... How versatile. Is there anything you can't do?

t_ch: 比如…跳起来？
t_en: Well, for starters, I can't jump...

t_ch: …我也不擅长。
t_en: ...I'm not very good at that either.

t_ch: 不要再夸我了，我会不好意思。
t_en: Omg, stop it~ You're making me blushhh~

t_ch: 接下来，我将担任天舶司司舵。
t_en: Nope. 'Tis why I'll totally take over the Sky-Faring Commission next.

t_ch: 打住吧。
t_en: ...Not on my watch.

t_ch: …会长曾帮助金人巷实现繁荣，是「金人巷的英雄」。不过即便是英雄，也不会永远被人拥戴。
t_en: The president is still a hero to Aurum Alley — he helped it achieve prosperity... but a hero will not be embraced forever.

t_ch: 而公司的做法我见过，他们讲究效率，在全银河中推行标准统一的商业模式。
t_en: I've seen the IPC's approach — they prioritize efficiency and a standard uniform business model across the galaxy.

t_ch: 我劝告过他们，生搬硬套未免有些太小看仙舟，但无奈本地商会又太过保守。
t_en: I warned them that their business model wouldn't work on the Xianzhou without adjustments... but the Merchant Guild's overly traditional mindset is a different obstacle.

t_ch: 我直说吧，如今的金人巷商会毫无胜算。
t_en: Let me put it bluntly — Aurum Alley Merchant Guild has no chance of succeeding today.

t_ch: 商会缺乏上谈判桌的资本。
t_en: The Guild lacks the necessary capital to get to the negotiating table.

t_ch: 公司能给的肯定比你们多，但是评审的标准并非只看财力。
t_en: The IPC does have more capital on hand, but the review criteria are not solely focused on financial resources.

t_ch: 两者都不怎么样，非得选一个。
t_en: Neither party is ideal, but one has to be chosen.

t_ch: 但是你们可以成为不同于商会，也不同于公司的第三种可能。
t_en: ...And yet all of you now represent a third choice — different from both the Merchant Guild and the IPC.

t_ch: 我给你们的建议是，去同功坊看看，店老板霄老大是「提出重组商会」的人。要是能够得到他的认可，你们肯定会受到更多商铺的支持。
t_en: My advice to you is to visit the shopkeeper Mr. Xiao. He is the one who proposed the reorganization of the Merchant Guild. If you can get his support, you will definitely receive support from the other merchants.

t_ch: 谢谢驭空大人！
t_en: Thank you, Madam Yukong!

t_ch: 司辰宫中还有事务要忙，接下来就看你们的了。让老家伙看清楚，你们才是新的英雄。
t_en: I have business to attend to in the Palace of Astrum — the rest is up to you. Convince the old shopkeeper, and you may just become the new heroes of Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 客人是来修什么东西的？敝店能打包票，将您的机巧修复如初。
t_en: Need something fixed? We guarantee that your ingenium will be good as new.

t_ch: 不过要是化外民的科技，我得看看是哪个世界的。
t_en: Of course, if it's outworlder technology, it'll depend on the world...

t_ch: 我不是来修东西的。
t_en: I didn't come to fix anything.

t_ch: 金人巷能恢复如初吗？
t_en: How's the revival of Aurum Alley coming along?

t_ch: 客人，直接说明目的吧。
t_en: ...I'd appreciate if you could state the reason for your visit today.

t_ch: 霄老大，我是商会的小秘书明曦，我们来是想谈……
t_en: Mr. Xiao, I'm Mingxi, the secretary of the Merchant Guild. We're here to talk about—

t_ch: 别谈了，我支持公司。
t_en: Forget it. I support the IPC.

t_ch: 能告诉我们理由吗？
t_en: Can you tell us why?

t_ch: 请回吧。
t_en: Please leave.

t_ch: 走就走。
t_en: Hmph... Rude!

t_ch: 你们商会做了什么过分的事吗？
t_en: Did the Merchant Guild do anything out of line?

t_ch: 我不记得了。
t_en: I don't recall.

t_ch: 我们先离开这里吧。
t_en: Let's leave for now.

t_ch: 几位请留步。
t_en: Wait — please wait a moment.

t_ch: 看来家父让各位为难了呀。我叫霄翰，这附近的人叫我小少爷。
t_en: It sounded like my father was being a little difficult... I'm Xiaohan — people around here call me Little Master.

t_ch: 看来你们几位就是让斯科特代表当众道歉的人吧。
t_en: If I'm not mistaken, you're the ones who made Representative Skott apologize in public?

t_ch: 看来我们已经出名了呀。
t_en: Our fame precedes us...

t_ch: 为金人巷挺身而出，有理有节，真是了不起。
t_en: You stood up for Aurum Alley with reason and integrity — I'm thankful to you.

t_ch: 怎么有人自称小少爷的？
t_en: Why would someone call themselves Little Master?

t_ch: 我可不是自称啊，离开仙舟有好些年了，回到这里后儿时伙伴还叫我「小少爷」。
t_en: Oh, hehe, I didn't give myself that name — it's what my friends have always called me. I only recently returned to the Xianzhou after many years away, but it seems like the name stuck!

t_ch: 请问为什么会突然找上家父呢？
t_en: May I ask why you wanted to speak with my father?

t_ch: 码头需要与不同商铺合作，我们想邀请令尊与码头达成合作……
t_en: We need the support of the merchants for the dock, and we wanted to see if your father could join us...

t_ch: 家父不是已经拒绝了吗？
t_en: Hadn't my father already refused?

t_ch: …我们听说霄老大要求「重组商会」，是来了解原因的。
t_en: We heard that Mr. Xiao proposed to "reorganize" the Merchant Guild, and we wanted to ask why.

t_ch: 这样吧，我帮你们想个办法。
t_en: I see... I'll help you figure something out.

t_ch: 太好了，令令令郎，不是，令堂？…哎！您父亲真的很固执。
t_en: Great! Uh... your father is a very tough nut to crack.

t_ch: 他们老一辈匠人都是这样油盐不进…对了，这样吧，你们可以来家父的店铺做学徒。
t_en: The older generation of craftsmen can be rather set in their ways... Here, how about this — why not join my father's shop as apprentices?

t_ch: 家父面子薄，要是有水平的学徒相求，他看到你们的诚意，会改变态度的。
t_en: My father is stern on the surface, but if he sees a capable apprentice and a sincere heart, he'll change his attitude.

t_ch: 要是他不答应呢？
t_en: What if he refuses?

t_ch: 我们要是擅自帮忙，他脸上更挂不住。
t_en: Well, it's better to beg forgiveness than ask permission. Let's just start you out and see what he says.

t_ch: 有种「心诚则灵」的感觉…
t_en: Things might just fall into place if we try hard enough.

t_ch: 诚心诚意总是有打动人的力量。
t_en: A sincere heart always has the power to impress people.

t_ch: 你真不是骗咱们去打白工吗？
t_en: You aren't tricking us into doing free work, are you?

t_ch: 我是真心诚意想要帮各位解决问题的。各位的实习工资，会由我来支付的。
t_en: I truly want to help you — and you'll receive compensation for your work.

t_ch: 走吧，我带你去做学徒。
t_en: So it's decided? Let's get moving then. Time to make you apprentices.

t_ch: 老爸，这位商会的朋友想来您这边做学徒，我带他们上手了啊。
t_en: Father, my friends from the Merchant Guild want to be your apprentices. I'll go ahead and show them the ropes.

t_ch: 胡闹！别对客人失礼。
t_en: Nonsense! I never said I needed more hands!

t_ch: 况且这同功坊的情况你又不是不知道，我们哪儿有钱雇新学徒？
t_en: Even if I did want to hire more people, I couldn't afford them. You know that better than I do.

t_ch: 所以我这不是替你找来了吗？
t_en: I can cover their salaries! Come on, it'd be rude to turn them away.

t_ch: 胡闹，修不好事小，{F#她}{M#他}这样的新手万一砸了客人东西，我还得照赔，岂不是砸了同功坊的招牌？
t_en: Don't be ridiculous — a green hand like {F#her}{M#him} won't be able to fix a thing... Plus, if {F#she}{M#he} breaks our customer items in the process, it'll be us taking the blame.

t_ch: 那你就别逃避，和人家好好聊聊。
t_en: If that's what you're worried about, just give 'em a little training.

t_ch: 我现在是学徒了，对吧？
t_en: So I'm an apprentice now, right?

t_ch: 我们是真的想和您聊聊。
t_en: We just want to help.
Hmph...

t_ch: 看来我老爸点头了，咱们开始吧？{NICKNAME}，恰巧有熟客送来个坏掉的机巧，要不咱们就露一手，修好这个。
t_en: That sounds like a "yes" to me. Let's get on with the job, shall we? {NICKNAME}, here's something from one of our regulars. Show him what you can do.

t_ch: 手挺巧…咳。
t_en: Hmm... not bad.

t_ch: 师傅，还有什么活要做？
t_en: Anything else I can help you with?

t_ch: 师傅和我们聊聊吧？
t_en: Are we friends now? Wanna have a chat?

t_ch: …不必了。
心里有数，眼里有活，我再帮你们找个活。
t_en: *sigh* He's hard to impress. I'll find you some more work.

t_ch: …唉，气死我了。
t_en: Ugh... what did I do to deserve this?

t_ch: 老爸，人家替咱们把活儿干了，咱们工钱可不能吝啬啊。
t_en: Just a reminder, Father — fair work for fair pay.

t_ch: 来来，{NICKNAME}，这边！这儿还有个玉兆你可以试试。
t_en: Over here, {NICKNAME}! See what you can do with this jade abacus.

t_ch: 老爸，你也别光在那里看着，过来指导指导学徒呀。
t_en: Don't just stand there, Father! Go teach your apprentice a thing or two.

t_ch: 好了好了！我已经清楚各位的来意了…求你们不要再碰客人送来维修的东西了！
t_en: Fine, I give in! I know why you're here. Just... please stop tinkering with my customers' items.

t_ch: 这就是「心诚则灵」吗？
t_en: We convinced him!

t_ch: 恐怕不是吧……
t_en: I think we just made him surrender...

t_ch: 不好意思，把工作台搞乱了。
t_en: Sorry if I made a mess of your workbench.

t_ch: 算了，你别再动就行了。
t_en: It's fine, just leave it as it is.

t_ch: 唉，想问什么就问吧。
t_en: *sigh* Ask what you wanted to ask.

t_ch: 你和会长很熟？
t_en: You and the president — you know each other well?

t_ch: 我也做了很久生意，会长算是老友。
t_en: Yes, he's an old friend.

t_ch: 你好像更加支持公司的做法？
t_en: You seem to be on the side of the IPC?

t_ch: 公司提出的方案存在很多可能性，但那个老东西只是过去留下的一潭死水。他想要的，不会实现。
t_en: I see a lot of potential in the Corporation's plan... but a certain old friend of mine lives in the past. Whatever vision he has — it won't come true.

t_ch: 你对商会有什么不满吗？
t_en: What's your issue with the Merchant Guild?

t_ch: 不满？不，没有的事。我只是固执而已。
t_en: Issue? It's less about issues, and more about stubbornness.

t_ch: 固执…对于仙舟人来说算是一种常态了。活过了几百年光阴，习惯了每天走在同一条道路上，干同样的活，与同样的人交谈…大部分人早已安于这样的生活方式。
t_en: We walk a single path, do the same things, and talk to the same people for centuries... A mind can become insensitive to change.

t_ch: 但论固执，会长那个老东西比我有过之无不及。他比我活得更久，曾经见过金人巷的盛况，那时这里星槎往来穿行，金人摩肩接踵。
t_en: However, compared to the president, my stubbornness is nothing. Back in his day, Aurum Alley was teeming with shuttling starskiffs and commercial aurumatons.

t_ch: 但罗浮仙舟一旦展开巡猎，就会远航其他星域，原本往来的商旅也就不再往来了。
t_en: But then the Luofu departed again on the Path of The Hunt. Our business partners couldn't follow, so they stopped trading with us.

t_ch: 你想复现这里的繁荣？
t_en: You want to restore the Alley to its former glory?

t_ch: 是会长。会长想要重现金人巷繁荣的旧时光。只是我们商铺都知道，是时代变了，没有任何人的努力可以扭转时代的变迁。
t_en: The president does — he wants to bring the old times back. Shop owners like me know that's impossible.

t_ch: 所以我接受公司的方案，金人巷可以消失，在罗浮之上还会有其他集市取代它的。
t_en: Therefore, I stand behind the IPC's plan. Aurum Alley will inevitably be replaced.

t_ch: 会长反对公司就是为了这个？
t_en: Is that why the president is against the IPC?

t_ch: 会长常常讲起，他想要重现那段繁荣的旧时光。
t_en: He always says the Alley deserves another chance.

t_ch: 是啊，这也是他顽固不肯放手的理由。他不想让金人巷变作公司的物流码头，他还盼着旅客们人来人往的景象再一次出现呢。
t_en: Indeed, which is why he won't let it go. He wants to see the streets teeming with tourists, not IPC cargo.

t_ch: 但时代变了，没有任何人的努力可以扭转时代的变迁。我接受公司的方案，金人巷可以消失，在罗浮之上还会有其他集市取代它的。
t_en: But times have changed — we can't sail against the current. That's why I stand behind the IPC's plan — Aurum Alley will inevitably be replaced.

t_ch: 可是在公司承诺的未来里，同功坊还会存在吗？
t_en: But is your shop part of the future that the IPC promised?

t_ch: …谁知道呢。同功坊多半是不会留下来的吧。
t_en: ...Hard to say. Chances are I'll have to sell it.

t_ch: 不过呢，我不会后悔的。好日子过去了。长生种得学会接受事实，接受短生种们改造自己的土地，把夜市变成货运码头……
t_en: Not the easiest decision to make, but I won't regret it. The good days are behind us — we must accept that short-life species will repurpose our lands, and that night markets are destined to become shipping docks...

t_ch: …接受那些只有短短几十年的野心家，确实胜过了我们这些活久了的老东西。
t_en: ...We must come to terms with the fact that short-life ambitions will reshape a world where things were thought to last forever...

t_ch: 霄老大，要不要试试与我们合作呢，小秘书正试着改变商会，我们和会长可不一样。
t_en: Mr. Xiao, why not try working with us? Mingxi is pushing for reforms within the guild. We're nothing like the president.

t_ch: …你们？改变商会？
t_en: Reforms?

t_ch: 他们曾在码头上与公司代表据理力争，我看到了商会变化的可能。
t_en: They made a lot of good points when arguing with the IPC at the dock. I do see possibilities for the guild to change.

t_ch: 金人巷的未来可不是二选一。
t_en: You don't have to choose between the president and the IPC.

t_ch: 加入我们，我们让你包赚不赔！
t_en: Join us and profit!

t_ch: 以商会现在的实力，还是公司赢面较大。
t_en: Given the state of the guild, my money is on the IPC winning...

t_ch: 不过，我都抛下了「金人巷必须存在」的执念，就绝不会否定你们胜利的可能性。在那之前，你们就放手试试吧！
t_en: ...but since I've accepted the worst-case scenario — Aurum Alley ceasing to exist — I can't see why it hurts to endorse your idea... and see how far it takes us.

t_ch: 同功坊愿意参加金人巷复兴计划，这肯定会影响很多人的。
t_en: Thank you! Now that you have joined our revival project, I'm sure many others will follow suit.

t_ch: 好啊，让我瞧瞧，你们能做到什么地步。
t_en: Mm... That's settled then. I'm curious to see what comes of your plan.

t_ch: 既然霄老大愿意参加金人巷复兴计划，事不宜迟，咱们现在就开始物流规划吧。
t_en: Now that Mr. Xiao is on board, his shop can be part of the logistics network we've been working on.

t_ch: 希望我们接下来也能合作愉快，各位辛苦了，我还有事就先回码头了。
t_en: I'll head back to the dock for now. Thanks for all the hard work by the way — hope things work out just as smoothly down the line.

t_ch: 商会为同功坊定制了推广方案，同功坊因而声名鹊起。
t_en: The merchant guild has established a promotional plan for Artistry Hardware, and hence did its fame begin to grow.

t_ch: 你们来得正是时候，小少爷正打算讲讲会议策略。
t_en: Right on time! Xiaohan is about to share his strategy for the upcoming hearing.

t_ch: 会长喜欢用他过去的经验和数据来说事，当然，我们也可以。
t_en: The president likes to quote past experiences and data. We can do that, too.

t_ch: 会长和驭空大人也到了，你准备好了吗？
t_en: The president and Madam Yukong are here. Ready?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 我也准备好了。
t_en: Let's do this.

t_ch: 我还没有准备好。
t_en: I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: 在正式交涉前，我们要确认一下手头的「材料」。
t_en: Before we start, let's review the documents we have.

t_ch: 金人巷商会营业额？
t_en: Turnover of Aurum Alley Merchant Guild?

t_ch: 这是金人巷复兴计划最直接的成果，更重要的是，它是金人巷恢复商业活力的证明。这部分金额将用作「金人巷商区招标」的保证金。
t_en: This is the most direct achievement of our revival project. More importantly, it's evidence that the Alley has regained its footing. The money will serve as the bidding deposit for Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 同功坊的营收分析？
t_en: Sales analysis of Artistry Hardware?

t_ch: 同功坊与码头合作以来，业务范围扩展，收入提升的同时，还获取了好的口碑。
t_en: Since Artistry Hardware partnered with the dock, its scope of operations has expanded, and its revenue and customer satisfaction have both increased.

t_ch: 许多顾客慕名而来，实际到店人数不降反升，这与会长的设想相悖。
t_en: As a result, we're seeing an uptick in customers visiting the shop — contrary to the president's assumptions.

t_ch: 金人巷历史交易量？
t_en: The historical trading volume of the Alley?

t_ch: 资料上不难看出，金人巷的辉煌是时代造就的。
t_en: The data suggests that the success of the old Aurum Alley was largely due to the economic circumstances of the past.

t_ch: 即便是最辉煌的时期，金人巷的交易量也几乎是与现在持平。
t_en: At its peak, the old Aurum Alley had a trading volume similar to what the Alley has today.

t_ch: 材料我都确认过一遍了。
t_en: I've looked over everything.

t_ch: 公开会议就要开始了。
t_en: The hearing is about to begin.

t_ch: 驭空用眼神锐利，似乎是在提醒你会议就要开始了，她不会偏袒你们其中任何一方。
t_en: Yukong's gaze is penetrating, as if she is reminding you that the meeting is about to begin. She will not be biased toward either side.

t_ch: 连烟笑而不语，等待着会议开始。
t_en: Lianyan smirks but says nothing, simply waiting for the meeting to start.

t_ch: 我谨代表天舶司，受邀主持金人巷商会的公开会议。小秘书，你们人到齐了吗？
t_en: I will be hosting this hearing on behalf of the Sky-Faring Commission. Mingxi, are your people all present?

t_ch: 我们到齐了。可是，会长人呢？
t_en: We're all here... but where's the president?

t_ch: 我就在这里。
t_en: I'm right here.

t_ch: …你是会长？
t_en: ...You're the president?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That is correct.

t_ch: 老朽我也不想公开示人的，但今日事关金人巷未来，我不得不出席。
t_en: I know my appearance is a little unsettling... If the hearing weren't of vital importance to the future of Aurum Alley, I wouldn't attend in person.

t_ch: 怎么，对面是一块铁疙瘩，你就害怕了？
t_en: Still, I didn't expect a reaction like this... Are you so afraid to converse with a piece of metal?

t_ch: 比起害怕，我更感觉到奇怪。
t_en: More surprised than afraid.

t_ch: 金人巷里有金人，很奇怪吗？
t_en: Surprised that there's an aurumaton in Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 小孩子看了都会做噩梦。
t_en: I hope there aren't any children here.

t_ch: 请你告诉小孩我是发声玩具。
t_en: *sigh* If there are any children here, just tell them I'm one of those talking toys...

t_ch: 作为玩具有些太惊悚了。
t_en: ...that's missing the rest of its body.

t_ch: 金人头，这也太酷了吧！
t_en: A talking aurumaton head? So cool!

t_ch: 你是懂行的人。
t_en: Glad you think so.

t_ch: 会议将大家召集起来，不是听你们闲聊的。会长，请阐述你对金人巷的规划与考虑。
t_en: Let me remind everyone that we are at a hearing. Mr. President, please share with us your vision for Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 明曦，该你们发言了。
t_en: Mingxi, please ready your statement.

t_ch: 我能理解孩子们对金人巷的热情，这一点我也一样，我们都想让这里更加繁荣。
t_en: I can tell that the opposing side is eager to see Aurum Alley thrive. Your passion resonates with me.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 但是你想要的繁荣，与我们想要的不同？
t_en: Since we share the same passion, where is it that we disagree?

t_ch: 你们想要的繁荣会消灭金人巷的传统与个性，那是我们承受不起的繁荣。
t_en: The prosperity under your proposal means diminishing the Alley's tradition and personality — we can afford neither.

t_ch: 公司计划将金人巷改造为物流中转仓，你们所作所为的本质也是如此。
t_en: The IPC plans to turn the Alley into a logistics hub. You are practically thinking the same.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 我们的做法和公司是有区别的！
t_en: Our proposal is different from the IPC's!

t_ch: 没错，公司接手金人巷之后，他们可能关闭「同功坊」，而我们就不会。
t_en: That's right. The IPC will close down Artistry Hardware if they take over. We would never do that.

t_ch: 我们用决策保护商铺，而公司只会为了决策牺牲商铺。
t_en: We protect businesses, the IPC sacrifices them for their own gain.

t_ch: 保护？牺牲？这不过是你们人类的漂亮语言，我只会看结果。
t_en: Protect? Sacrifice? I find that humans rarely understand these concepts correctly. The outcome is what truly matters.

t_ch: 一旦物流主导金人巷，同功坊这样的商铺很快会消亡，甚至不需要公司出手。
t_en: Once the Alley is turned into a logistics hub, shops like Artistry Hardware won't be able to sustain themselves, regardless of what the IPC does.

t_ch: （他完全将我们和公司划上等号了……）
t_en: (He is convinced that the IPC and us are the same...)

t_ch: 过度开发只会影响游客体验，导致实际来到街道的游客减少，这就是现实。
t_en: The reality is — overdevelopment can only adversely impact customer experience, making the Alley less appealing.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 这是你用数据得出的结论？
t_en: Do you have data to support your statements?

t_ch: 历史数据远比你们的说辞更有说服力，<color=#dbc291ff>你们最好也学会使用数据</color>。
t_en: Historical data has proved my point. <color=#dbc291ff>It is the reality, not rhetoric that matters</color>.

t_ch: （这句话好像不太对劲……）
t_en: (I sense a hole in his logic...)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 游客并没有减少！
t_en: There are actually more customers coming to the Alley!

t_ch: 这份数据记录了同功坊与码头合作以来的营收变化，以及到店游客数量的变化。
t_en: This record reflects the change in Artistry Hardware's turnover and the increase in customers since it partnered with the dock.

t_ch: 不降反升，这不可能…难道你们在虚张声势？
t_en: The shop does better than it used to? Impossible... you must be bluffing.

t_ch: 你比我更熟悉小秘书，她会用假数据来说服你吗？
t_en: You know Mingxi better than I do. Would she deceive you with fake data?

t_ch: 这只是个例，还不足以说服我。
t_en: The sample size is too small. I'm not convinced.

t_ch: 会长，我要是你，我会将个例变成普遍案例。
t_en: If I were you, Mr. President, I would think of ways to translate the success of that one sample into something that can benefit everyone.

t_ch: 不能够为商铺们着想的商会，是没有意义的。
t_en: A guild that doesn't proactively help businesses has no reason to exist.

t_ch: 你们没有经历过我所经历的时代，所以你们才不理解「我的坚持」。
t_en: You have not witnessed what I have seen, and so you fail to understand my insistence.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你的说法不就是在倚老卖老吗？
t_en: You can't just rely on your age and experience...

t_ch: 时间能说明很多事情，年轻人。
t_en: There are many things that only time can tell, young one.

t_ch: 曾经的金人巷选择了「我的坚持」，它才会创造如今无法企及的商业奇迹。
t_en: The old Aurum Alley achieved a commercial miracle that has not yet been replicated. It did so by following my way of doing things.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你的意思是说现在的金人巷无法超越当时的金人巷？
t_en: Are you suggesting that the Alley is a shadow of its former self?

t_ch: 没错，如今金人巷游人数量没有达到巅峰时期的十分之一，那时金人巷车水马龙、摩肩接踵，是真正的「罗浮第一夜市」。
t_en: That is correct. The number of customers visiting the shops was ten times what it is today. The Alley was alive, and was credited as the best night market on the Luofu.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这一点毋庸置疑</color>。
t_en: Things were better in the old days — <color=#dbc291ff>of that I am certain</color>.

t_ch: （这句话好像不太对劲……）
t_en: (I sense a hole in his logic...)

t_ch: 出示
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你所描述的过去并不客观。
t_en: You've been looking at the past through rose-tinted glasses.

t_ch: 它充分证明了即便是最辉煌的时期，金人巷的交易量也几乎是与现在持平的。
t_en: In the golden era you described, the old Aurum Alley had a trading volume similar to today.

t_ch: 你们把飞来飞去的机巧鸟也算进去了。
t_en: You included the delivery cycranes in your math.

t_ch: 没错，这又如何？
t_en: We did. So what?

t_ch: 过去不能和现在直接对比。那时的环境和现在完全不……
t_en: You can't draw simple parallels between then and now using absolute numbers. The trading volume you mentioned may not look impressive today, but it was huge relative to...

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 继续说，我在听。
t_en: Go on, I'm listening.

t_ch: …你想说「过去的做法不能直接适用于现在」，因为「那时的环境和现在完全不一样」——我终究还是得出了你们的论点。
t_en: ...I see what you are saying. While criticizing you for making flawed comparisons, I myself also made the mistake of believing that what worked in the past could be copied to serve the present.

t_ch: 会长，在我看来，金人巷不是用来缅怀过去的幻影。
t_en: Mr. President, the way I see it, you shouldn't regard the Alley as a souvenir from the past.

t_ch: 金人巷是人的金人巷，只要人还在变化，金人巷就应该变化。
t_en: Aurum Alley should embrace change, as its residents do.

t_ch: 看来会议至此已经得出了结论，散会吧。
t_en: It seems we have reached an agreement. The hearing concludes here.

t_ch: 如果人类无法从历史里学会经验，那金人会教给你们。
t_en: If humans are unable to draw wisdom from history, aurumatons can teach them.

t_ch: 追问
t_en: (Continue questioning)

t_ch: 你怎么可以断定我们没有从历史中学会经验呢？
t_en: What makes you think we didn't draw any wisdom from history?

t_ch: 你们从历史中学到了不少，学会了嘴硬与诡辩，却对真正的教训视而不见。
t_en: You have indeed learned — about wordplay and logic traps — while ignoring the true lessons.

t_ch: 出示。
t_en: (Present evidence)

t_ch: 你看看这个。
t_en: Take a look at this.

t_ch: 这能说明什么？请不要浪费时间。
t_en: What am I looking at? Please don't waste our time.

t_ch: 我能理解孩子们对金人巷的热情，这一点我也一样，我们都想让这里更加繁荣。
t_en: I can tell that the opposing side is eager to see Aurum Alley thrive. Your passion resonates with me.

t_ch: 公司计划将金人巷改造为物流中转仓，你们所作所为的本质也是如此。
t_en: The IPC plans to turn the Alley into a logistics hub. You are practically thinking the same.

t_ch: 过度开发只会影响游客体验，导致实际来到街道的游客减少，这就是现实。
t_en: The reality is — overdevelopment can only adversely impact customer experience, making the Alley less appealing.

t_ch: 你们没有经历过我所经历的时代，所以你们才不理解「我的坚持」。
t_en: You have not witnessed what I have seen, and so you fail to understand my insistence.

t_ch: 曾经的金人巷选择了「我的坚持」，它才会创造如今无法企及的商业奇迹。
t_en: The old Aurum Alley achieved a commercial miracle that has not yet been replicated. It did so by following my way of doing things.

t_ch: 如果人类无法从历史里学会经验，那金人会教给你们。
t_en: If humans are unable to draw wisdom from history, aurumatons can teach them.

t_ch: 先是怒怼公司代表，现在又让会长哑口无言，{NICKNAME}你好厉害。
t_en: First challenging an IPC rep, and now winning over the president of the guild! {NICKNAME}, you're pretty good!

t_ch: 这次没有当众道歉的环节吗？
t_en: I was looking forward to another public apology.

t_ch: 这就算了吧……
t_en: That won't be necessary...

t_ch: 运气好而已，多亏大家的帮助。
t_en: I got lucky, and it was a collective effort.

t_ch: 明曦和小少爷的材料都很关键。
t_en: The materials that Mingxi and Xiaohan provided were super useful.

t_ch: 依我对会长的了解，他现在才算是放心将金人巷的未来交给你们。
t_en: Knowing the president, I think he's now ready to entrust you with the future of the Alley.

t_ch: 会长其实另有目的？
t_en: He wasn't really against us?

t_ch: 金人巷里不少老字号都信任会长，他们对会长的「支持」是你们复兴计划中的阻力。
t_en: The president commands the trust of many reputable shops in the Alley. Their support for him was likely to be a big obstacle to your rejuvenation project.

t_ch: 会长要求你和他进行公开会议辩论，那些拒绝交流的商铺们才有机会了解到你们的理念。
t_en: His invitation to debate this matter publicly was a chance for the more difficult business owners to hear from you directly.

t_ch: 会长真是用心良苦。
t_en: He is indeed very wise.

t_ch: 驭空大人你在过度解读。
t_en: You are overinterpreting, Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 就当是我想多了吧。
t_en: If you say so.

t_ch: 我其实挺不好意思的，毕竟金人巷的敌人不是会长，应该是星际和平公司。
t_en: I almost feel bad for him. Aurum Alley's enemy should be the IPC, not the president.

t_ch: 要我说，也许公司也不是敌人呢？
t_en: What if... the IPC isn't an enemy?

t_ch: 公司不打算参加竞标了吗？
t_en: Have they decided to drop out of the bidding?

t_ch: 公司当然还会竞标，我会堂堂正正拿下金人巷的经营权。
t_en: Of course not. We'll win the operating rights to Aurum Alley fair and square.

t_ch: 你忘记了斯科特代表了吗？
t_en: You still remember Skott, right?

t_ch: 他已经被调离仙舟了…接下来的谈判将由我亲自处理。
t_en: He's been transferred elsewhere. I will be handling things from now on.

t_ch: 你是公司的人？！
t_en: ...You're with the IPC!?

t_ch: 霄翰是公司在长乐天的业务代表。
t_en: Xiaohan is the IPC's operation rep in Exalting Sanctum.

t_ch: 你小子！
t_en: You snake!

t_ch: 你小子在这里埋伏我呢？
t_en: You back-stabbing little...

t_ch: 我早就猜到你是公司的人。
t_en: Can't say I'm surprised.

t_ch: 我原本仅仅是想帮各位扳倒现任会长，后来我发现，我们是志同道合的朋友。
t_en: My original plan only involved helping you overthrow the current president... but as we progressed, I saw there was more common ground than I'd anticipated.

t_ch: 既然大家都意识到码头的重要性，为何不向前一步，将金人巷打造成物流中心呢？
t_en: Since we all agree that the dock is vital to Aurum Alley's development, why not take things further and turn the Alley into a full-blown logistics hub?

t_ch: 想想金人巷停满星际货船的样子，这里会成为享誉银河的商业港口，而不是普通的集市。
t_en: Imagine cargo ships from all corners of the galaxy docking and departing. This place will be transformed into a renowned commercial port, instead of another run-of-the-mill market.

t_ch: 你的想法很有意思。
t_en: I'm intrigued.

t_ch: 我不认同你的想法。
t_en: That isn't progress.

t_ch: 等到公司拿下金人巷之后，希望大家还能聚在一起复兴金人巷。
t_en: When the IPC wins the bidding, I'm sure we can continue to work together for a brand-new Aurum Alley.

t_ch: 喂！我们还没打算输给你啊。
t_en: Hey! Not so fast, it's still anyone's game.

t_ch: 看来是我太过自信了，请各位继续加油，我们谈判桌上见。
t_en: ...Maybe I'm getting ahead of myself. In that case, keep up the good work. I'll see you at the negotiation table.

t_ch: 小少爷霄翰离开了码头，金人巷复兴计划的下一阶段开始了。
t_en: Xiaohan leaves the dock, the next phase of Aurum Alley's revival has begun.

t_ch: 多亏了各位的努力，金人巷码头每天都在全力运转，和上次一样，我安排了码头工人将原本闲置的码头清理了出来，升级了「码放货物」的规模。
t_en: Thanks to your help, the dock is running at full capacity. Just like last time, I told the workers to clear out areas that weren't utilized and use them to store extra cargo.

t_ch: 这下码头不仅能够支持与更多的商家合作，还打开了另外一条去往港口的通路，这样「物流规划」也会轻松不少，让我们一起为金人巷复兴努力吧！
t_en: We now have the additional capacity to work with more businesses and have also opened a new route leading to the port. With streamlined logistics planning, I think we'll see a revitalized Alley in no time!

t_ch: 之前我拜托素裳去说服尚滋味的燕翠师傅和码头合作，最近这件事有了进展。
t_en: Some time ago, I asked Sushang to have a chat with Yancui to see if we could secure Spices Supreme as the next client for the dock. I've heard Sushang has made some progress.

t_ch: 素裳需要有人帮助，所以我想麻烦你过去看看。
t_en: But she could use some extra hands. Would you mind helping her out?

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 辛苦了，我在准备竞标的文件，暂时抽不开身。
t_en: Thanks so much. I would have helped her myself, but I've been drowning in paperwork in preparation for the bidding.

t_ch: 你为什么不去？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 我在准备竞标的文件，暂时抽不开身，只好拜托你了。
t_en: Sorry, I would have helped her myself, but I've been drowning in paperwork in preparation for the bidding.

t_ch: 这对我有什么好处吗？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 商会的福利肯定少不了你的，拜托啦。
t_en: The guild will make it worth your while. Pleaaaase~

t_ch: 素裳姑娘就在尚滋味等你，快去吧。
t_en: Sushang is waiting for you at Spices Supreme. Go find her when you have the time.

t_ch: 这里，这里！
t_en: Over here!

t_ch: 请客的人来了！
t_en: Right on time to pay the bill!

t_ch: 我听素裳讲了你们在金人巷做的事情，好厉害。
t_en: Sushang told me all about what you did to help the shops in the Alley. Very impressive!

t_en: *sigh* I wish Spices Supreme would step up their game, though. It'd be a dream come true if I could order takeout from the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 难道尚滋味不打算做外卖？
t_en: Huh? They don't do takeout?

t_ch: 对吧，明明是金人巷的金字招牌。
t_en: Unbelievable, right? The food is so good.

t_ch: 难道不是派送距离太远了？
t_en: Is the Alchemy Commission outside the delivery area?

t_ch: 你说的很有道理，不过物流机巧鸟能够跨越洞天送货。
t_en: Perhaps, but cycranes can make deliveries between delves.

t_ch: 为什么我不能在列车上吃到呢？
t_en: Why can't I order from the Express?

t_ch: 你这属于是超出配送范围了。
t_en: That's definitely out of their delivery area.

t_ch: 尚滋味的燕翠师傅之前是「会长派」，现在她听了明曦的计划后打算和码头合作，是个好机会。
t_en: Chef Yancui used to be on the president's side. After she heard about Mingxi's plans, she decided to cooperate with the dock. It's a good opportunity.

t_ch: 啊，尚滋味的外卖！
t_en: Ah! There's finally hope for Spices Supreme takeout!

t_ch: 不止这个，小秘书说这也是宣传金人巷的机会。
t_en: Not only that, putting Spices Supreme on the map would increase interest in the Alley as a whole.

t_ch: 所以我提出建议，让金人巷商会与尚滋味联动，只要购买尚滋味的食盒，就可以随机获得白露大人的绘影图形怎么样？
t_en: To get us started, I've proposed a crossover between the Merchant Guild and Spices Supreme — buy a meal box and get a visiograph of Bailu!

t_ch: 这是白露和尚滋味联动！
t_en: Cool! A Bailu-Spices Supreme crossover!

t_ch: 等、等等！听起来怪怪的，要是被丹鼎司的人和长老们知道，一定会怪我不自尊身份的！
t_en: Wait a second! I'm not sure about that idea... If the elders find out, they'll say I'm tarnishing my image or something!

t_ch: 我和燕翠师傅谈好了，活动期间，你可以在尚滋味白吃白喝。
t_en: Yancui has agreed to offer you free meals throughout the crossover.

t_ch: 我同意。
t_en: Happy to help.

t_ch: 我感觉不妙哇。
t_en: Hmm, I don't know...

t_ch: 怎么，你是在小看我吗？

t_en: No, I think the high demand might crash the servers...

t_ch: 我怕联动太火，燕翠忙不过来。
t_en: No, I think the high demand might mean Yancui has to grow an extra arm...

t_ch: 是哦，而且持明龙尊为金人巷的菜肴代言…总觉得有失身份。
t_en: Hmm, you have a point. Also, I'm not sure it's a good idea for the High Elder of the Vidyadhara to appear on takeout boxes...

t_ch: 你放心，我和燕翠师傅谈好了，活动期间，你可以在尚滋味白吃白喝。
t_en: Yancui has agreed to offer you free meals throughout the crossover.

t_en: Now, would you mind giving me the low-down on this crossover event?

t_ch: 会长，他们到了。
t_en: Mr. President, they've arrived.

t_ch: 老朽这副身体有失远迎，希望你们理解。
t_en: You'll forgive me for not shaking hands.

t_ch: 理解理解。
t_en: No worries.

t_ch: 你不是没有身体吗？
t_en: You don't have hands to shake.

t_ch: 所以让你理解理解！
t_en: ...My point exactly.

t_ch: 看来这次不需要别人转述了。
t_en: Good to speak to you directly.

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Indeed.

t_ch: 小秘书第一次把你们带到我面前的时候，说老实话，我从未有过期待，你们不过是金人巷每天都会路过的好心人。
t_en: I must admit I didn't expect much when Mingxi first brought you to me. I thought you were just another group of strangers who meant well but wouldn't make a difference.

t_ch: 我本以为解决了码头的租金问题之后，你们就打算离开了。但是你们不仅留了下来，还说服了更多的店家加入你们，最后也说服了我。
t_en: To my disbelief, not only did you manage the lease, but you stayed to convince others — including me — that our cause is one that is worth fighting for.

t_ch: 即便是到现在我也很难想象，你们将如何竞标金人巷…但这也并非不可能！
t_en: Even now it's hard for me to imagine how you are going to win the bidding. As slim a chance as we have, there is still hope.

t_ch: 金人巷就交给我们吧。
t_en: We got this.

t_ch: 要是我输了，也别怪我哦。
t_en: Don't blame me if we end up losing.

t_ch: 我不会怪你的。我用我漫长生命见证过无数像你这样寿数短暂的人类做出的奇迹。即便失败，我也会牢牢铭记你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I won't. I've seen enough miracles in all the years I've lived — miracles achieved by short-life species. Even if you fail, you still have my gratitude, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 别再煽情了，再讲下去会输的。
t_en: Don't go getting mushy on us.

t_ch: 我就讲最后一句。
t_en: One last word from this old piece of metal:

t_ch: 去吧，我就在这金人巷里，等着你们从司辰宫捎回好消息。
t_en: Go, and may good tidings come with you when you return.

t_ch: 小少爷为人谦恭有礼，平易近人，实在是让人很难想象他和斯科特代表一起共事。
t_en: It never struck me that someone as humble and kind as Xiaohan would work together with the likes of Skott.

t_ch: 在这之前，我总是用斯科特的形象去想象公司的一切，总以为他们盛气凌人、手段肮脏。
t_en: Before I met Xiaohan, I always imagined everyone at the IPC would be like Skott — overbearing and dodgy.

t_ch: 但现在至少我明白了一件事，公司并不只有一张面孔。
t_en: Now I understand, the IPC has many faces.

t_ch: 前面就是司辰宫，你准备好了吗？
t_en: Palace of Astrum is up ahead. Ready?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 我也准备好了。
t_en: Let's do this.

t_ch: 我还没有准备好。
t_en: I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: 在正式竞标前，我们要确认一下手头的「材料」。
t_en: Before we place our bid, let's go over our key points again.

t_ch: 金人巷商会竞标资金？
t_en: The Guild's bidding deposit?

t_ch: 这是金人巷复兴计划中最直接的成果，更重要的是，它是金人巷商业活力的证明。
t_en: This is the most tangible achievement of our Alley revival project. It demonstrates that the Alley still has plenty of life in it.

t_ch: 我们取得足够的资金，才有底气和公司共同竞标。
t_en: Having enough funding to back up our bid is key to competing with the IPC.

t_ch: 尚滋味的联动活动？
t_en: The Spices Supreme crossover event?

t_ch: 尚滋味推出「特色联动套餐」后，来到金人巷的游客数量增加了不少。
t_en: Because of the crossover meal deals, the Alley has seen an uptick in customers.

t_ch: 更重要的是，它证明了作为「美食街」的金人巷，在如今依然有十足的竞争力。
t_en: It goes to show that the Alley still lives up to its name as one of the best food destinations.

t_ch: 金人巷商铺的意向调查？
t_en: The Aurum Alley business opinion poll?

t_ch: 会长收集的商铺意向调查，它整理了商铺们对不同经营理念的倾向。
t_en: The president conducted a poll to gather the shops' opinions on the future of the Alley.

t_ch: 如今有更多的商铺们支持金人巷商会，并且看好金人巷复兴计划。
t_en: More and more shops have voiced their support for the guild and expressed their confidence in our revival project.

t_ch: 材料我都确认过一遍了。
t_en: I've looked over everything.

t_ch: 走吧，公开会议就要开始了。
t_en: Let's go. The bidding is about to begin.

t_ch: 驭空目光坚定，此时不与你对话是为了避嫌，聪明的你肯定能够领会。
t_en: Yukong's gaze remains steadfast. She's not talking with you right now to avoid arousing suspicion, but if you're smart enough, you should pick up on what the situation entails.

t_ch: 霄翰看上去信心满满，还是把交锋留在谈判桌上吧。
t_en: Xiaohan seems pretty confident. You should save the confrontation for when the debate begins.

t_ch: 我谨代表天舶司，受邀主持金人巷商区竞标会议。各方代表均已到齐，现在开标评审。
t_en: On behalf of the Sky-Faring Commission, I am invited to host the public bidding of the Aurum Alley commercial district. With members of all parties present, the proceedings may begin.

t_ch: 请各方代表提交投标文件，天舶司将审核经营范围、出资比例、注册资金等相关材料。
t_en: I hereby ask all parties to submit your support documents for evaluation. The documents should include scope of business, ratio of contributions, and registered capital.

t_ch: 天舶司确认完各方的投标文件之后，按照招标文件的评标办法开始审核工作。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission confirms all parties' bidding documents and bidding evaluations commence.

t_ch: 不出意料，公司与金人巷商会成为了留下的中标候选人。
t_en: To no one's surprise, the IPC and the Merchant Guild are the only two contenders left standing.

t_ch: 接下来，请中标候选人，向天舶司评标审查会解释金人巷之后的经营理念。
t_en: Representatives of all parties, please be ready to brief the review committee on your business plans.

t_ch: 算是我安排的特别要求吧，我会给你们自由讨论的时间，希望你们能充分碰撞观点。
t_en: By personal request of the committee, a discussion session will be added to allow all participants to raise and answer questions.

t_ch: 原本竞标是没有吵架的环节吗？
t_en: So, debates are not usually required in public bidding?
